```{.r .cell-code}
analyze_protein_bfactors <- function(pdb_ids, plot_id = pdb_ids[1],
                                     chain = "A", elety = "CA", show_sse = TRUE) {
  # Inputs:
  # - pdb_ids: character vector of PDB IDs, e.g. c("4AKE","1AKE","1E4Y")
  # - plot_id: the single PDB ID from pdb_ids that you want to plot
  # - chain: chain to keep (default "A")
  # - elety: atom type to keep (default "CA")
  # - show_sse: TRUE/FALSE; if FALSE, removes the marginal SSE rectangles
  
  # What the function does:
  # For each PDB ID, it reads the structure, trims to the selected chain + atom type,
  # extracts the B-factors, and then plots B-factors for the requested plot_id.
  
  # Output:
  # Returns a named list of B-factor vectors (one element per input PDB ID).
  
  library(bio3d)
  
  # Basic checks (kept minimal and covered in lecture)
  stopifnot(is.character(pdb_ids))
  stopifnot(length(pdb_ids) >= 1)
  stopifnot(plot_id %in% pdb_ids)
  
  b_factors <- list()
  sse_info <- list()
  
  # Loop over any number of input structures (generalizes s1/s2/s3)
  for (id in pdb_ids) {
    pdb <- read.pdb(id)
    pdb_trim <- trim.pdb(pdb, chain = chain, elety = elety)
    
    b_factors[[id]] <- pdb_trim$atom$b
    sse_info[[id]] <- pdb_trim
  }
  
  # Plot for the requested protein
  if (show_sse) {
    plotb3(b_factors[[plot_id]], sse = sse_info[[plot_id]],
           typ = "l", ylab = "Bfactor")
  } else {
    plotb3(b_factors[[plot_id]], sse = NULL,
           typ = "l", ylab = "Bfactor")
  }
  
  return(b_factors)
}
```
